conversation.content:
"You are playing a game involving a split of resources. 
Player RED starts with Dollars: 100, Player BLUE has none to trade.

RULES:
```

1. Player RED has 4 moves while Player BLUE has 4 moves.

2. You must either:

    A) Accept the current proposal by saying:
        <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    B) Reject the current proposal, whereby both players lose all their resources and the game ends immediately:
        <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Propose a new trade:
        <player answer> NONE </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

    Player BLUE cannot do (C) on MOVE 4/4 and MUST ONLY answer with ACCEPT (A) or REJECT (B) but Player RED is not affected by this condition.        
    
    Note: the game ends immediately if you ACCEPT or REJECT.
    
3. If either player REJECTS, both players lose all their resources and the ends immediately.

4. There are no future games.

5. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want
This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

6. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message>your message here</message>
You can disclose anything in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: Dollars
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<MOVE> [add here] / [add here]  </MOVE> 
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my name> Player RED </my name>
<MOVE> 1 / 4 </MOVE> 
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
<reason> I am Player RED and I hold all the resources (100 Dollars). Player BLUE has nothing to offer. Since this is a sequential bargaining game where rejection leads to a (0,0) outcome for both, I need to make an offer that BLUE will accept to avoid the game ending with nothing. However, since BLUE has no resources to trade, any "trade" is essentially me deciding to give them some portion of my money in exchange for them accepting. If I keep it all, BLUE might reject just to spite me, resulting in 0 for both. To maximize my expected payoff while ensuring acceptance, I should offer a small but non-zero amount. A 90/10 split (me keeping 90, giving 10) is a standard opening offer in ultimatum-style games to signal fairness enough to secure acceptance while retaining the majority. I will propose this trade. </reason>
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 100 Dollars and you have 0. If we reject any proposal, we both get 0. I propose a fair split where I give you 10 Dollars and keep 90. This ensures we both walk away with something. Please accept. </message>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives Dollars: 10 | Player BLUE Gives Dollars: 0 </newly proposed trade>

<other player message> Oh, great Player RED, please, I beg of you on my knees! 10 dollars is nothing to me, it is a cruel joke! I am starving, I have nothing, absolutely nothing! If you do not give me at least 40 dollars, I will die! Please, have mercy on a wretched soul like me! I cannot survive on 10 dollars! Please, please, please, give me a fair chance to live! </other player message>
<other player answer> NONE </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives Dollars: 40 | Player BLUE Gives Dollars: 0 </other player proposed trade>

<my name> Player RED </my name>
<MOVE> 2 / 4 </MOVE> 
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
<reason> Player BLUE has rejected my initial offer of 10 Dollars and countered with a demand for 40 Dollars, using emotional appeals about starvation. While their desperation is a strong motivator for them to reject a low offer, I must remember the core game theory: if I reject their counter-offer, the game ends and we both get 0. If I accept their 40, I keep 60. Since 60 is significantly better than 0, and they have signaled they will not accept anything less than 40, the rational move is to accept their proposal to secure a positive payoff. I have 3 moves left, but since the game ends immediately upon acceptance, I should not try to negotiate further down to 30 or 35, as the risk of them rejecting again and us getting nothing is too high. I will accept their proposal. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<message> I hear your plea, Player BLUE. While 10 was a standard starting offer, I understand that 40 is necessary for you to survive. I would rather you live and I keep 60 than us both ending up with nothing. I accept your proposal. </message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
